original_title: Development of a pyrogallol-formaldehyde resin column for one-step pre-purification of <sup>68</sup>Ge/<sup>68</sup>Ga generator eluates: Enhanced [<sup>68</sup>Ga]Ga-DOTA-TATE radiolabeling yield by selective removal of tetravalent cationic impurities
title: Purifying Ga-68 eluate before synthesis
blurb: A resin column that removes metal impurities from 68Ge/68Ga generator output before 68Ga-DOTA-TATE synthesis, boosting yield. Simple chemistry with real payoff if the resin is stable and cost-effective.

original_title: Development of a Hydraulic-Drive Automatic Column Chromatography System for High Precision Isotope Analysis
title: Automated isotope chromatography
blurb: A hydraulic-drive column system for high-precision isotope separations. The snippet has no detail on what problem it solves, so hard to say if anyone actually needs this.

original_title: Apt19s-functionalized 3D-printed PCL/58S bioactive glass scaffolds via thiol-maleimide click chemistry enhance hBMSC adhesion and osteogenic differentiation.
title: 3D-printed scaffolds with aptamer coatings
blurb: Thiol-maleimide click chemistry adds Apt19s aptamers to 3D-printed PCL/bioactive glass, improving cell adhesion and osteogenic differentiation. It's a working proof of concept, but whether aptamers beat other surface modifications remains to be shown.

original_title: Development and Advantages of O-(Carboxymethyl)-L-tyrosine-Based PSMA-Targeting Theranostics for Prostate Cancer.
title: PSMA theranostics on a tyrosine scaffold
blurb: An O-carboxymethyl-L-tyrosine linker for PSMA-targeting diagnostics and therapy. The design advantages are outlined on paper, but clinical data will decide whether they matter.

original_title: Recycling-compatible electroplating of enriched tin-119 targets for the production of antimony-119 for targeted Auger electron therapy
title: Recyclable Sb-119 production via electroplating
blurb: Electroplating enriched tin-119 targets that can be recovered after Sb-119 extraction, making the process reusable. Auger therapy is still early overall, but reducing production waste is sensible groundwork if it scales.

original_title: Bioorthogonal Click Chemistry Engineered Bioinks for 3D Bioprinting in Osteochondral Regeneration and Osteoarthritis Therapy: A Translational Review.
title: Click-chemistry bioinks for joint repair
blurb: A review of bioorthogonal click chemistry in 3D-printed scaffolds for osteochondral regeneration and osteoarthritis. Most is still preclinical, and engineering a good scaffold is easier than the biology of actually regrowing cartilage.

original_title: Investigation of radiochemical procedures for the sequential separation of Pu, Am, and Cm in radioactive waste with alpha spectrometry
title: Separating plutonium, americium, and curium from radioactive waste
blurb: A radiochemical procedure for sequential separation of these three actinides from waste streams, detected by alpha spectrometry. Practical for handling legacy waste, though the separation efficiency is probably matrix-dependent.

original_title: Recent advances in bioconjugation via CuAAC click reaction: Synthesis and bio-applications - ScienceDirect.com
title: Recent advances in bioconjugation via CuAAC click reactions
blurb: A survey of copper-catalyzed azide-alkyne cycloaddition for bioconjugation and its applications. Standard chemistry, but worth reading if you're setting up a new conjugation route.

original_title: Electroless semi-automatic synthesis of <sup>225</sup>Ac-labeled PSMA-derivatives for clinical application and evaluation of rechelation capability
title: Semi-automatic Ac-225 PSMA synthesis without electrochemistry
blurb: A procedure for labeling PSMA-targeting molecules with Ac-225 without electrochemistry, set up to run semi-automatically. The rechelation data is what matters, and if the label actually stays put in vivo, clinical production becomes more feasible.

original_title: One-Pot Synthesis of Functionalized Coumarin Fluorophores Enables Rapid Access to Live-Cell Bioorthogonal Labeling and Microenvironmental Sensing Agents
title: One-pot coumarins for live-cell bioorthogonal labeling and sensing
blurb: A one-pot synthesis of functionalized coumarins that can label live cells orthogonally and sense local environment. Coumarins are solid for fluorescence microscopy, though quantum yields and photostability dictate whether this is actually faster than existing routes.

original_title: [<sup>68</sup>Ga]Ga-NOTA-ATH001 positron emission tomography as a general tool for non-invasive detection of platelet derived growth factor receptor beta in mesenchymal cells
title: Ga-68 NOTA-ATH001 for PET imaging of PDGFRβ
blurb: A Ga-68 radiopharmaceutical targeting platelet-derived growth factor receptor beta for PET imaging of mesenchymal cells. Ga-68's half-life and NOTA's chelation stability are both favorable, so the main question is whether target uptake and clearance actually give usable images.

original_title: Enhanced pre-therapy dosimetry for SSTR PET: A head-to-head preclinical assessment of [<sup>89</sup>Zr]Zr-DFO-TOC and [<sup>89</sup>Zr]Zr-DFO-TATE
title: Zr-89 SSTR ligands compared for pre-therapy dosimetry
blurb: A preclinical comparison of Zr-89 DFO-TOC and Zr-89 DFO-TATE for imaging before somatostatin receptor therapy. Zr-89's longer half-life is ideal for pre-therapy dosimetry, and the head-to-head approach should reveal any kinetic differences.

original_title: In situ identification of substrates of the protein tyrosine phosphatase PTP1B using site-specific photo-crosslinking
title: Photo-crosslinking to trap PTP1B with its substrates
blurb: Site-specific photo-crosslinking to capture PTP1B substrates in their native context before the interaction dissociates. Tyrosine phosphatases are fast and transient, so a crosslinking trap is a reasonable tool for identifying the actual targets, though you'd want to verify the adducts are catalytically relevant.

original_title: First-in-Class CD146-Targeting Peptide Probes for Noninvasive PET Imaging of Melanoma.
title: CD146-targeting peptide probes for melanoma PET imaging
blurb: A peptide-based radiopharmaceutical targeting CD146 for non-invasive PET imaging of melanoma. The first-in-class label suggests the target isn't well-established for melanoma imaging yet, so it's worth asking whether this outperforms existing approaches.

original_title: Genetic Code Expansion, Enzymatic Modification, and C-Terminal Labeling Enable Facile Production of Highly Modified α-Synuclein.
title: Heavily modified alpha-synuclein via genetic code expansion and enzymatic labeling
blurb: Using genetic code expansion, enzymatic modification, and C-terminal labeling to produce highly functionalized alpha-synuclein. This is a preprint, so it hasn't been peer-reviewed yet, but stacking modifications this way could be useful for probes and structure-function studies.

original_title: Conjugates of Heme and Intrinsically Disordered Peptides toward the Development of Synthetic Artificial Oxygen Carriers
title: Heme-peptide conjugates as synthetic oxygen carriers
blurb: Coupling heme to intrinsically disordered peptides as a scaffold for synthetic oxygen-binding proteins. It's an interesting construct, but oxygen carriers have strong existing competition, and you'd need to show these resist autoxidation better than the alternatives.

original_title: Chemical Proteomics Reveals a Novel Long-Acting Maleimide Algaecide Targeting Glyceraldehyde-3-Phosphate Dehydrogenase for Cyanobacterial Bloom Control.
title: A maleimide targeting GAPDH to kill cyanobacterial blooms
blurb: Chemical proteomics found a long-acting maleimide that targets GAPDH in cyanobacteria. Targeting the glycolytic enzyme for bloom control is an interesting angle, though the snippet doesn't say how it compares to existing algaecides.

original_title: Library-based, multiplexed strategy for mapping protein interaction networks via crosslinking.
title: Crosslinking libraries for faster protein interaction mapping
blurb: A library approach runs multiple crosslinkers in parallel to map protein interactions. More coverage faster, but false positives scale with the number of probes, so you'd want to see the signal-to-noise floor before committing your samples.

original_title: Dual-Chelator conjugates for [(68)Ga]Ga- and [(177)Lu]Lu-based PSMA Radiotheranostics.
title: One chelator for both Gallium-68 and Lutetium-177 PSMA
blurb: A dual-chelator design coordinating Ga-68 and Lu-177 for PSMA imaging and therapy. Streamlines pairing diagnostics with treatment, though isotope swaps usually trade off binding kinetics or clearance, tradeoffs the paper would need to quantify.

original_title: Personalized Radionuclide Therapy via Avidin-Biotin Pretargeting: Biodistribution and Dosimetry of 177 Lu-DOTA-biotin with AvidinOX™ in Solid Tumors
title: Injecting an artificial receptor into tumors to guide Lu-177 therapy
blurb: Avidin injected directly into solid tumors creates a binding site for circulating Lu-177-biotin conjugates, steering the dose to the lesion. Three patients' biodistribution and dosimetry looked as planned, but the sample is too small to compare against standard PSMA therapy.

original_title: Antibody-drug conjugates in gynecologic malignancies: breakthroughs, challenges, and future directions.
title: Antibody-drug conjugates for gynecologic cancers
blurb: A review of ADCs in ovarian, cervical, and endometrial malignancies, covering clinical reality and where the field still struggles. Worth reading if you're designing a gynecologic ADC program, though the heavy lifting stays at the bench.

original_title: Importance of an alkyl group inserted into the DOTA backbone for reducing renal radioactivity levels of <sup>111</sup>In-labeled antibody fragments
title: An alkyl chain in DOTA cuts kidney uptake of In-111 antibodies
blurb: Inserting an alkyl group into the DOTA backbone reduced renal retention in In-111 labeled antibody fragments. Cleaner dosimetry in principle, but the effect size and generalizability to other isotopes and scaffolds would need independent verification.

original_title: A Phase 1, Dose-escalation Study of [225Ac]-FPI-2068 in Adult Patients With Advanced Solid Tumours
title: Phase 1 trial of Actinium-225 FPI-2068 in solid tumors
blurb: Dose escalation study of Ac-225-FPI-2068 from AstraZeneca in patients with advanced solid tumors. Early stage, so the main signals to watch are safety and tolerability.

original_title: Development of a Fully Automated FASTlab 2 Process for [ 18 F]AlF Radiolabelling of NOTA Peptide Conjugates
title: Automating Aluminium-18F labeling of NOTA peptides
blurb: A FASTlab 2 process for Al-18F radiochemistry on NOTA peptide conjugates. Mild aqueous conditions and rapid synthesis make it practical for peptides that don't tolerate conventional radiometals or the organic solvents and heat they typically require.
venue: Journal of Labelled Compounds and Radiopharmaceuticals

original_title: Overall Survival with [(177)Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 Versus [(177)Lu]Lu-PSMA I&T: A Propensity Score-Matched Real-World Analysis.
title: Real-world survival with Lu-177 PSMA-617 versus PSMA I&T
blurb: Propensity-score matching comparing overall survival between Lu-177 PSMA-617 and PSMA I&T in actual prostate cancer patients. Useful for clinicians picking between them, though matching can't fully erase the confounding baked into real-world cohorts.

original_title: Dosimetry and pharmacokinetics of [(223)Ra]Ra-dichloride alpha therapy in prostate cancer patients with bone metastases.
title: Dosimetry and pharmacokinetics of Ra-223 in bone-metastatic prostate cancer
blurb: Measurements of Ra-223 biodistribution and organ doses in prostate cancer patients with bone metastases, the main indication for Ra-223 alpha therapy. Adds clinical detail to the pharmacology, though the takeaway depends on whether doses match prior calculations or suggest you need to retool patient selection.

original_title: Mucus Anchoring Protein (MAP) – Antibody Conjugates: A Strategy to Enhance Antiviral Efficacy
title: Anchoring antibodies in mucus keeps them at the infection site
blurb: Respiratory viruses start at the airway mucosa, so locking neutralizing antibodies there with a mucus-anchoring protein should extend their effectiveness. Whether it beats free antibodies in practice is another question from this snippet.

original_title: Validation of Manual In-House Synthesis, Imaging, Biodistribution, and Dosimetry of Tb-161 PSMA-617 for mCRPC Patients in a Tertiary Health Care Center: A Proof-of-Concept Study
title: Tb-161 PSMA therapy that you can make in-house
blurb: Terbium-161's Auger electrons target micrometastases, and this proof-of-concept shows you can synthesize and dose it locally even in resource-limited settings. It's early, but on-site production could open PSMA therapy to places that can't import isotopes.

original_title: [(68)Ga]Ga-DOTA-DP-UBI 29-41: A Novel (68)Ga-Labeled Ubiquicidin 29-41 Derivative Containing d-Proline for Bacterial Infection PET Imaging.
title: Imaging bacterial infections with Ga-68-labeled ubiquicidin
blurb: Ubiquicidin peptides target bacterial infections, and this d-proline variant labeled with Ga-68 improves stability for PET imaging. It's mostly a labeling chemistry study, though using antimicrobial peptides for infection detection is a reasonable approach.

original_title: Dual-trigger hyaluronic acid nanoprodrug incorporating a 2-nitrobenzenesulfonyl linker for CD44-targeted and glutathione-responsive drug delivery.
title: Nanodrugs that activate only once they reach tumor cells
blurb: Hyaluronic acid guides the nanodrug to CD44-positive tumors, and a nitrobenzenesulfonyl linker keeps the cargo inactive until intracellular glutathione breaks it loose. Two safety checks instead of one, though whether the extra complexity translates to better efficacy is another question.

original_title: NEPC Study: An Exploratory Safety and Efficacy Study With PSMA, SSTR2 and GRPR Targeted Radioligand Therapy in Metastatic Neuroendocrine Prostate Cancer.
title: Triple radioligand therapy for neuroendocrine prostate cancer
blurb: Neuroendocrine prostate cancer expresses PSMA, somatostatin, and GRP receptors, so this Phase 1 combines all three radioligands at once. The biological rationale makes sense, but multitarget approaches rarely deliver the efficacy their preclinical logic suggests.

original_title: Tannic acid-crosslinked layer-by-layer nanocomposite coatings enhance mechanical stability and pre-osteoblast response in porous polymer-based scaffolds for bone tissue engineering.
title: Toughening bone scaffold surfaces with tannic acid coatings
blurb: Tannic acid crosslinks a layer-by-layer nanocomposite coating that stiffens porous polymer scaffolds and improves pre-osteoblast response at the same time. Materials fine-tuning rather than a breakthrough, though matching mechanical properties to native bone is one of the real bottlenecks in scaffolds.

original_title: Cell-Friendly 3D Bioprinting of Gelatin-Norbornene Bioink via Thiol-Ene Crosslinking Enabled by Interfacial DTT Delivery from Alternating Support Layers.
title: Bioprinting with thiol-ene click chemistry and local DTT delivery
blurb: Gelatin-norbornene bioinks crosslink via thiol-ene click triggered by DTT from sacrificial support layers, keeping the local environment cell-friendly. It avoids UV phototoxicity, though whether the mechanical properties match traditional bioinks is less clear.

original_title: The evolving landscape of enzymatic technologies for precision synthesis of antibody-drug conjugates.
title: How enzymes are making ADC synthesis more precise and predictable
blurb: Sortase, tyrosine ligases, and other enzymatic methods offer site-specific attachment to antibodies, replacing the lysine-bashing that yields heterogeneous ADC batches. It's mostly reviewing existing methods rather than breaking new ground, but the shift toward enzymatic precision is real.

original_title: A Dose Finding Study of [177Lu]Lu-DOTA-TATE in Newly Diagnosed Glioblastoma in Combination With Standard of Care and in Recurrent Glioblastoma as a Single Agent.
title: Using somatostatin radioligand therapy to hit glioblastomas
blurb: Glioblastomas express somatostatin receptors, so this Phase 1 tests Lu-177 DOTA-TATE combined with standard chemo in newly diagnosed patients and alone in recurrent cases. The rationale is reasonable, but somatostatin therapy has been more effective in neuroendocrine cancers, so the jump to glioma is speculative.

original_title: Panitumumab labeled with Auger electron-emitting 197gHg/197mHg: cytotoxicity on human breast cancer cells and tumour and normal tissue uptake in mice with EGFR-overexpressing breast cancer xenografts
title: Auger-emitting mercury labels on anti-EGFR antibodies for breast cancer
blurb: Panitumumab labeled with Hg-197g/m delivers high-LET Auger electrons to EGFR-overexpressing breast cancer cells. Preclinical work shows the approach works, though whether mercury is biocompatible enough for patients is less certain.

original_title: Advances in computational anthropomorphic phantoms and Monte Carlo-based dosimetry: A review
title: Computational phantoms and Monte Carlo dosimetry, reviewed
blurb: A survey of where computational anthropomorphic phantoms stand in Monte Carlo-based dosimetry. It's a methods review, so mostly of interest if you're building these systems, but the field moves fast enough that catching up on the state of the art has some value.

original_title: 68Ga-PSMA PET/CT in biochemically recurrent prostate cancer: association with genomic data
title: 68Ga-PSMA imaging tied to genomic markers in recurrent prostate cancer
blurb: Looking at whether PSMA PET/CT findings correlate with genomic data in biochemically recurrent prostate cancer. The angle is sound if the sample size supports it, but you'd want to see the effect sizes before the pattern becomes actionable.
venue: European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging

original_title: Effects of Perioperative 177 Lutetium-Labeled Targeted Radionuclide Therapy on Inflammatory Response and Intestinal Recovery in Colorectal Cancer
title: 177Lu-CEA therapy in the perioperative window for colorectal cancer
blurb: A retrospective study of 177Lu-labeled anti-CEA radionuclide therapy given around surgery, tracking inflammatory response and intestinal recovery. The dataset is likely small and retrospective, so the safety conclusions would be preliminary until prospective data comes in.

original_title: Pharmacokinetics and dosimetry of radiolabeled FAP-targeting single-domain antibody 4AH29 in Göttingen minipigs
title: Single-domain antibody targeting FAP shows PK and dosimetry in minipigs
blurb: Pharmacokinetic and dosimetric profile of a FAP-targeting single-domain antibody in Göttingen minipigs, showing what to expect in dose escalation. Minipigs are a reasonable preclinical model, but the translation to humans is never guaranteed, especially for an antibody fragment.

original_title: Organelle-Specific Interactome Mapping via Enzyme-Responsive Cross-Linking and Proximity Labeling.
title: Mapping interactomes inside organelles with enzyme-triggered crosslinks
blurb: A proximity labeling method using enzyme-responsive chemistry to capture interactions within specific organelles instead of averaging whole-cell data. It's a solid technical tool for resolving compartment-specific networks, though most labs don't need that resolution for the questions they're asking.

original_title: Engineering the Future of ADCs in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer.
title: ADC engineering strategies for non-small cell lung cancer
blurb: A survey of how antibody-drug conjugate design is being applied to NSCLC. Mostly a review summarizing the field, so it's a catch-up read if you've been out of the loop on recent ADC approaches.

original_title: Automated cGMP Radiosynthesis of [18F]NOTA-ABY-030 with Aluminum Fluoride on the GE FASTlab Automated Synthesis Platform
title: Automated [18F]NOTA-ABY-030 synthesis on GE FASTlab for EGFR imaging
blurb: A cGMP radiosynthesis of an [18F]NOTA-ABY-030 tracer for EGFR imaging in head and neck squamous cell carcinoma, automated on the GE FASTlab platform. Automation of radiopharmaceutical synthesis looks clean on paper, but whether FASTlab actually beats traditional manual methods in real clinic throughput is worth benchmarking.

original_title: Sub-study of Belantamab Mafodotin (GSK2857916) as Monotherapy and in Combination With Nirogacestat, Pomalidomide, and Dexamethasone in Participants With RRMM
title: Belantamab mafodotin combinations in relapsed multiple myeloma
blurb: A phase 1/2 trial testing the anti-BCMA ADC belantamab mafodotin as monotherapy and in combination with nirogacestat, pomalidomide, and dexamethasone in relapsed/refractory myeloma. Combination trials like this are exploratory, and figuring out which drug is doing what gets messy fast.

original_title: Hansoh B7-H3 ADC Wins Phase III SCLC Trial - AllSci
title: Hansoh B7-H3 ADC hits phase III endpoints in small cell lung cancer
blurb: Hansoh's B7-H3-targeting ADC cleared phase III in small cell lung cancer. Phase 3 wins are good news, though the real impact depends on the comparator arm and magnitude of survival benefit, both of which the headline doesn't provide.

original_title: Surfactant-Mediated Buchwald–Hartwig Coupling of Aliphatic Amines for the Synthesis of DNA-Encoded Libraries
title: Buchwald-Hartwig coupling of aliphatic amines in water for DNA libraries
blurb: A surfactant-mediated approach to Buchwald-Hartwig coupling of aliphatic amines in aqueous conditions, enabling larger DEL syntheses. It's an optimization of existing chemistry, so the real value is whether it actually speeds up DEL production or just makes the method greener on paper.

original_title: Development of a novel radiopharmaceutical kit for radiolabeling with Lu-177 for application in breast cancer treatment
title: A Lu-177 labeling kit for trastuzumab
blurb: A theranostic radiopharmaceutical for HER2+ breast cancer using Lu-177-labeled trastuzumab with four chelating agents under test. Chelator optimization is standard work; the checkpoint is whether any delivers clean labeling and favorable in-vivo kinetics.

original_title: GSK and Hansoh’s ADC clinches late-stage win, teeing up regulatory submission in China - BioSpace
title: GSK and Hansoh's ADC clears late-stage trial
blurb: The partnership's ADC hit its late-stage endpoint and will be submitted for regulatory approval in China. Efficacy data aren't in the headline, though, so the actual magnitude of the win is still under wraps.

original_title: Antibody-drug conjugates in colorectal cancer: current landscape and future perspectives from clinical trials.
title: Antibody-drug conjugates in colorectal cancer
blurb: A review of ADC trials in colorectal cancer and the landscape ahead. Useful for tracking which targets have reached the clinic and which ones stayed preclinical, which is the real measure of progress.

original_title: Sub-study of Belantamab Mafodotin (GSK2857916) in Combination With Isatuximab in Participants With RRMM
title: Belantamab and isatuximab in relapsed multiple myeloma
blurb: A Phase 1/2 trial of belantamab mafodotin (anti-BCMA ADC) with isatuximab (anti-CD38) in relapsed/refractory myeloma. Combination is plausible, but belantamab's tox profile in single-agent work has been concerning, so this is testing whether the partnership improves tolerability.

original_title: Hydropersulfide-Mediated Stereoselective and Regioselective Hydrothiolation of P-alkynes
title: Stereoselective hydrothiolation of p-alkynes with hydropersulfides
blurb: A synthetic route using hydropersulfides to add thiols to p-alkynes with stereo- and regioselectivity control. Methods paper in Organic Letters, so the value hinges on substrate scope and whether it beats existing hydrothiolation approaches.

original_title: A Phase I/II Study of Sacituzumab Govitecan Plus Berzosertib in Small Cell Lung Cancer, Extra-Pulmonary Small Cell Neuroendocrine Cancer and Homologous Recombination-Deficient Cancers Resistant to PARP Inhibitors
title: Sacituzumab govitecan and berzosertib in small-cell lung cancer
blurb: A Phase 1/2 trial of sacituzumab govitecan (anti-TROP-2 ADC) plus berzosertib (ATR inhibitor) in SCLC, extra-pulmonary neuroendocrine cancer, and HRD-resistant tumors. ATR inhibition should boost the ADC and overcome PARP resistance in theory, but whether the combination is actually tolerable is the bet.

original_title: Enzymatically Crosslinked Hybrid Hydrogels from Ultrasound-Decellularized Spinal Cord Meninges and Hydrophilic Silk Fibroin for Neural Tissue Engineering.
title: Hybrid hydrogels from spinal cord meninges and silk for neural tissue engineering
blurb: Enzyme-crosslinked hybrid hydrogels combining decellularized spinal cord meninges (for neural ECM) with silk fibroin (for mechanical support). The chemistry is sound, but the real test is whether axons actually grow through the construct and bridge a spinal cord lesion.

original_title: Automated cGMP Radiosynthesis of [(18)F]NOTA-ABY-030 with Aluminum Fluoride on the GE FASTlab Automated Synthesis Platform.
title: [18F]NOTA-ABY-030 synthesis on automated GMP platform
blurb: Automated cGMP radiosynthesis of [18F]NOTA-ABY-030 (anti-HER2 Fab) using aluminum fluoride on a GE FASTlab platform. Platform compatibility is necessary for clinic scaling, but whether the synthesis holds up across different sites and operators is the next question.

original_title: First Patient Treated With PSMA-Targeted Actinium-225 Alpha Emitter in mCRPC - Targeted Oncology
title: First patient dosed with Ac-225-PSMA for metastatic prostate cancer
blurb: A patient with mCRPC received an Ac-225-PSMA alpha-emitter, marking the first clinical use of Ac-225 therapy in prostate cancer. Supply has long been the bottleneck, so this proves concept, though one patient teaches you nothing about efficacy or the tox profile at scale.

original_title: Peptide crosslinking by cytochrome P450 enzymes: Diversity, discovery and developments.
title: Peptide crosslinking via cytochrome P450 enzymes
blurb: A review of P450-mediated peptide crosslinking, the diversity of reactions available, and where the field is headed. Most remain biochemistry tools rather than practical bioconjugation methods, which is the real measure of whether this matters outside the lab.

original_title: Cross-Species Quantitative Benchmarking of SSTR2 Radioligands in Fresh-Frozen Brain Sections: An Adaptable Framework for Radiopharmaceutical Development.
title: How SSTR2 radioligands stack up across species
blurb: Quantitative benchmark for comparing SSTR2 ligand candidates in fresh brain tissue from multiple species. Lets you pick the best one before committing to full preclinical studies. In-vitro binding does not always predict in-vivo behavior, though.

original_title: Crossover Analysis from the PLUDO PR21 Trial of Lutetium-177 vs. Docetaxel - Kim Chi - UroToday
title: Lutetium-177 PSMA beats docetaxel in the PLUDO crossover
blurb: Real-world crossover analysis showing Lu-177 PSMA-617 outperforms docetaxel in mCRPC. Crossover designs let you see patient preferences, but later treatments look worse because patients deteriorate over time. Read the sequencing controls before taking it at face value.

original_title: [Expert consensus on the perioperative management of liver cancer treated with Yttrium-90 selective internal radiation therapy (2026 edition)].
title: Expert guidance on Y-90 SIRT perioperative care
blurb: Expert panel consensus on pre- and post-procedure management for Y-90 selective internal radiation therapy in hepatocellular carcinoma. Updated for 2026 with newer toxicity data and imaging guidance. Consensus papers trail the actual research, but they're useful for operationalizing what works.

original_title: Preclinical Evaluation of [18F]JNJ-1: A Novel Positron Emission Tomography Ligand Targeting AMPAR/TARP γ8
title: A PET tracer targeting AMPAR-TARP γ8
blurb: JNJ-1 labeled with fluorine-18 as a positron tracer for AMPAR complexes containing TARP γ8 subunits. Preclinical data in rodent brain shows specific uptake. Whether it outperforms existing AMPAR tracers or opens a clinical imaging window is still unclear.

original_title: A novel radionuclide probe targeting FN-EDB: a new strategy for radioactive iodine-refractory differentiated thyroid cancer.
title: FN-EDB targeting for iodine-refractory thyroid cancer
blurb: Radiolabeled probe targeting FN-EDB, an oncofetal antigen in thyroid cancer stroma. Oncofetal antigens are common in tumors, so the real question is specificity versus noise. Still early, and efficacy in patients is unknown.

original_title: GSK's Antibody-drug Conjugate for Small-cell Lung Cancer Meets Primary Endpoint in Late-stage China Study - marketscreener.com
title: GSK's ADC for small-cell lung cancer hits primary endpoint in China
blurb: Phase 3 trial showing their antibody-drug conjugate meets primary endpoint in SCLC in China. It's company-sponsored data under different regulatory standards, and the numbers are GSK's own. Wait for independent data from Western populations.

original_title: GSK sees phase 3 win for Hansoh-partnered ADC in China as validating its ‘priority asset’
title: GSK and Hansoh's ADCs keep winning phase 3 trials in China
blurb: Their partnered antibody-drug conjugates continue posting phase 3 wins in Chinese trials, validating GSK's prioritization. Both companies have large bets on these, and all data is China-only. Wins in Western markets would carry more weight.

original_title: Efficacy and outcomes of [(177)Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 in patients with mCRPC treated with or without concurrent ARPI: a real-world single-center analysis.
title: Lu-177 PSMA in mCRPC: androgen inhibitors improve outcomes
blurb: Real-world single-center analysis of whether adding androgen receptor pathway inhibitors boosts Lu-177 PSMA-617 outcomes in mCRPC. Retrospective data with heterogeneous patient selection. Whether the benefit comes from the combination or from patient factors is hard to separate.
venue: Eur J Nucl Med Mol Imaging

original_title: FPI-2265 (225Ac-PSMA-I&T) and Olaparib for Patients With Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer (mCRPC)
title: Actinium-225 PSMA plus PARP inhibitor for prostate cancer
blurb: Phase 2 trial combining FPI-2265, an Ac-225-labeled PSMA targeting agent, with olaparib in mCRPC. The logic is sensible—alpha particle damage plus PARP inhibition—but it's single-arm and early. Signal versus noise is still unclear.

original_title: ‘Target-high but uptake-defective’ state identified in ADC resistance - BioWorld News
title: ADC resistance without losing the target
blurb: Researchers identified an ADC resistance mechanism where tumor cells maintain high antigen expression but fail to internalize the drug conjugate. It's a real mechanism and potentially tractable. Whether it predicts clinical resistance or whether you can reverse it is still open.

original_title: Dynamic Changes in Tumor Burden in Patients Undergoing PSMA RLT.
title: Tumor burden changes during PSMA radioligand therapy
blurb: A clinical observation of how tumor burden shifts as patients go through PSMA radioligand therapy, the standard treatment for advanced prostate cancer. Useful for knowing what to expect, though whether the response predicts long-term survival is the real question.

original_title: Radioligand Therapy in 2026 and the Role of PSMA PET Imaging - Steven Finkelstein - UroToday
title: Radioligand therapy and PSMA imaging in 2026
blurb: An expert take on where radioligand therapy stands mid-decade and how PSMA PET imaging fits into the clinical decision tree. Commentary rather than new data, but worth reading if you're staying current on the field.

original_title: Curved Vertebroplasty System Combined with Targeted Radionuclide Therapy for Tumor-Related Pathological Vertebral Fractures
title: Curved vertebroplasty plus radionuclide therapy for cancer spine fractures
blurb: A comparison of a curved vertebroplasty system combined with radium-223 or strontium-89 against conventional vertebroplasty with the same isotopes for tumor-induced spine fractures. The setup makes mechanical sense, though the clinical benefit over the standard approach depends on how much the geometry actually matters.

original_title: PSMA PET Imaging of Recurrent Prostate Cancer
title: Gallium-68 PSMA imaging for recurrent prostate cancer
blurb: A Phase 2 trial of Ga-HBED-iPSMA imaging in men with recurrent prostate cancer, run by Memorial Sloan Kettering. Gallium-68 tracers have practical advantages over fluorine-based ones for same-day imaging, though this is still mid-phase development.

original_title: Development of angiostatin based radiopharmaceutical as a sensitive tool for detection of ATP synthase during acute vascular damage
title: An angiostatin-based tracer for acute vascular damage
blurb: A radiopharmaceutical using angiostatin to detect ATP synthase in acute vascular injury. It's a methods paper focused on tool development rather than clinical use, and whether detecting ATP synthase specifically in vascular damage matters clinically isn't clear yet.

original_title: Development of a Commercial Manufacturing Process for Ganaplacide Phosphate
title: Scaling ganaplacide phosphate for commercial production
blurb: A manufacturing process for ganaplacide phosphate, the sort of work that has to happen before you can make drug substance at scale. Published in Organic Process Research & Development, so the focus is yield, cost, and reproducibility rather than efficacy.

original_title: Xeno-Free Peptide-Functionalized Hydrogels Support hiPSC Encapsulation and In Situ Differentiation into Structurally Mature Cardiomyocytes
title: Peptide hydrogels grow mature cardiomyocytes without Matrigel
blurb: A xeno-free, fully-defined hydrogel made with cyclic RGD peptides that lets hiPSCs differentiate into structurally mature cardiomyocytes in 3D, replacing Matrigel without the batch variation. The real test is whether the cells behave like real myocardium once you measure electromechanical properties.

original_title: Microgel-templated cluster-crosslinked hydrogels for low-hysteresis soft electronics.
title: Low-hysteresis hydrogels for soft electronics with microgel templates
blurb: A method for making hydrogels with low hysteresis using microgels as templates for cluster crosslinking, aimed at soft electronics and wearable applications. The novelty is in the gel structure itself, though whether it scales or shows up in an actual wearable is another question.

original_title: Dual-Targeted Radionuclide Therapy with 161 Tb Instigates Anticancer Immunity in “Cold” Murine Prostate Tumor
title: Terbium-161 dual targeting triggers immunity in cold tumors
blurb: Dual-targeted terbium-161 therapy in mouse prostate cancer activates anti-tumor immunity even in immunologically cold tumors. It's preclinical work so no human data yet, but the immunological angle is worth tracking if Tb-161 ever makes it to the clinic.

original_title: A novel approach to radioembolization treatment planning using a hybrid [99mTc]Tc-MAA contrast enhanced SPECT/CT
title: Hybrid SPECT/CT planning for radioembolization dosimetry
blurb: A hybrid approach to radioembolization planning that combines contrast-enhanced CT with technetium-99m MAA SPECT to avoid spatial misregistration and account for disease progression between baseline and treatment. It's an incremental improvement in a mature technique, though whether it changes clinical outcomes beyond reducing retreatment is still open.
venue: European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging

original_title: Advances in PD-L1 Targeted Molecular Imaging Radiotracers Research: From Preclinical Exploration to Clinical Application
title: Imaging PD-L1 expression from bench to bedside
blurb: A review of molecular imaging agents targeting PD-L1, tracking development from preclinical work to clinical use. The field moves fast, so whether these specific candidates outlast the next iteration is hard to judge from one review snapshot.

original_title: Tunable, High-Relaxivity Gd(III)-Conjugated Lipoic Acid Hydrogels for Magnetic Resonance Imaging.
title: Gadolinium-lipoic acid hydrogels as tunable MRI contrast
blurb: Gd(III) conjugates embedded in lipoic acid hydrogels, with relaxivity tunable by formulation changes. Hydrogel-based MRI contrast is well-established, though, so the bar is whether this beats what's already in the clinic.

original_title: Development of a Freeze-Dried Bevacizumab Kit for 99m Tc Radiolabeling and Preclinical Evaluation In Vitro and in Normal Mice
title: Bevacizumab kit for Tc-99m radiolabeling
blurb: Bevacizumab formulated as a lyophilized kit for easy radiolabeling with Tc-99m, designed for tumor imaging. Still preclinical, but the practical value is a ready-to-use version of an antibody already FDA-approved.

original_title: PET molecular imaging of in vivo brain metabolism, synaptic density and glial reactivity changes after neural stem cell transplantation on a rat model of ischemic stroke
title: Neural stem cell transplant shifts brain metabolism in stroke
blurb: Multiple PET metrics tracking changes in brain metabolism, synaptic density, and glial reactivity following neural stem cell transplantation in a rat stroke model. Several readouts at once is useful, but which of these shifts actually predicts recovery is still missing.
venue: European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging

original_title: Breaking New Ground in Oncology: FDA Acceptance of Vepdegestrant NDA Marks a Milestone for PROTAC Therapies in Breast Cancer.
title: FDA accepts a PROTAC therapy for breast cancer
blurb: Vepdegestrant, an estrogen receptor degrader, has cleared FDA review for its new drug application. Whether it outperforms existing endocrine therapies depends on trial results still pending.

original_title: Review of Glypican-3-Targeted Imaging Agents for Hepatocellular Carcinoma
title: Glypican-3-targeted imaging for hepatocellular carcinoma
blurb: A review of glypican-3-targeted imaging agents for HCC, covering a marker overexpressed in 70-85% of tumors but silent in normal liver. Reviews map the field, but the real question is whether the best candidate here changes clinical practice.

original_title: Key Tips For Patenting Antibody-Drug Conjugate Inventions - Law360
title: Patenting antibody-drug conjugate inventions
blurb: Legal guidance from Law360 on ADC patent strategy and the patent landscape. It tracks patent behavior more than the underlying science, but useful if you're watching the IP space.

original_title: A Standard Machine-Learning Virtual Screening Pipeline Produces Candidates Indistinguishable From Random Drug-Like Compounds: A Quantitative Audit Using COX-2 As Case Study
title: Machine learning virtual screening doesn't beat random, COX-2 audit shows
blurb: ML screening trained well on retrospective data (Pearson R = 0.756) but failed to beat random compounds in prospective docking against the real target. That's the kind of result that should make you question whether the pipeline works at all.

original_title: Clinical Laboratory Evaluation of the Biodistribution of a Radiolabeled Antimicrobial Peptide for Precision Radiopharmaceutical-Guided Radionuclide Therapy in Severe Pneumonia Among Immunocompromised Patients with Cancer
title: Radiolabeled antimicrobial peptide for pneumonia in cancer patients
blurb: Clinical evaluation of a radiolabeled antimicrobial peptide for imaging and guided therapy in immunocompromised cancer patients with pneumonia. Early work in a vulnerable population, so biodistribution and safety matter more than efficacy claims at this stage.

original_title: Dynamic effects of radioactive iodine therapy on gut microbiota and metabolites in patients with papillary thyroid cancer.
title: Radioactive iodine therapy reshapes the gut microbiota in thyroid cancer
blurb: I-131 therapy causes dynamic changes in gut microbiota composition and metabolite production in papillary thyroid cancer patients. Whether these shifts are clinically meaningful or just a byproduct of the radiation remains unclear.

original_title: Prostate Specific Membrane Antigen (PMSA)-Based PET Imaging of High Risk Prostate Cancer
title: PSMA PET for high-risk prostate cancer
blurb: Phase 2 trial with 18F-DCFPyL, a PSMA-targeted PET tracer from NCI. Whether better imaging actually changes treatment decisions or prognostication compared to current standards is still unproven.

original_title: Artificial Intelligence for Discovery in Life Sciences
title: AI for bioconjugate chemistry
blurb: The application of machine learning to bioconjugate design and discovery. Without details from the source material, it's hard to know what specific advances this represents.

original_title: Measurement of a panel of 21 steroids in a quantitative assay in human plasma, adipose tissue, and fecal samples using ultra-high-performance liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry
title: Measuring 21 steroids across tissues in one assay
blurb: A 21-steroid panel quantified in parallel across plasma, adipose, and fecal samples using UHPLC-MS/MS, far beyond what clinical labs typically measure. Steroids ionize differently and interfere with each other, so the assay had to be custom-built for the whole metabolic network.

original_title: Novel antibody-drug conjugate eliminates residual cancer cells in majority of patients with B-cell ALL - UT MD Anderson
title: ADC clears residual disease in B-cell ALL
blurb: A novel antibody-drug conjugate eliminated detectable residual disease in a majority of B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia patients. Follow-up duration and long-term remission status aren't in the news release, so durability is still an open question.

original_title: JMIR report: machine learning accelerates radiopharmaceutical drug discovery, optimizes personalized dosimetry - EurekAlert!
title: Machine learning accelerates radiopharmaceutical discovery and patient dosimetry
blurb: A JMIR report on using ML to speed radiopharmaceutical development and optimize patient-specific dosing. Single-center data so far, so cross-institutional validation will show whether these gains hold up.

original_title: A Study of SGN-B6A Versus Docetaxel in Previously Treated Non-small Cell Lung Cancer
title: ADC versus chemo in pretreated NSCLC
blurb: Phase 3 testing sigvotatug vedotin, an ADC, against standard docetaxel in patients who've already had one line of treatment. It's a targeted agent versus non-selective chemotherapy, so the real question is whether specificity translates to better outcomes.

original_title: A Study of Disitamab Vedotin With Other Anticancer Drugs in Solid Tumors
title: HER2 ADC plus tyrosine kinase inhibitor in solid tumors
blurb: Phase 2 combining disitamab vedotin and tucatinib, two different approaches to HER2, in HER2-positive breast and gastroesophageal cancers. Hitting the same target two ways might sharpen efficacy, but dual mechanisms usually stack the side effects.

original_title: LLM-Enhanced Multimodal Fusion of SPECT Radiomics and Clinical Data for Predicting 131I Therapeutic Response in Differentiated Thyroid Cancer
title: ML predicts I-131 response in thyroid cancer
blurb: An LLM-enhanced model combining SPECT radiomics with clinical variables to forecast which thyroid cancer patients will respond to radioactive iodine therapy. Whether the added ML layer outperforms conventional statistical fusion is the key test.

original_title: HuR degrader knocks down BRAF in cancer
title: HuR degrader reduces BRAF protein
blurb: A HuR protein degrader lowers BRAF levels by disrupting HuR-mediated stabilization of BRAF mRNA. It's an indirect angle rather than hitting BRAF directly, though whether that buys resistance protection is untested.

original_title: Direct Stereospecific Deoxyamination of Alcohols Enabled by Li-Mediated SuFEx
title: Stereospecific alcohol deoxyamination via SuFEx
blurb: A reaction converting alcohols to amines while retaining stereochemistry, using sulfur(VI) fluoride exchange with lithium. Primarily a methods advance in Organic Letters, useful for synthesis groups building aminated scaffolds.

original_title: Development of a new <sup>18</sup>F-labeled PET tracer for potential application to HDAC2 neuroimaging
title: An 18F PET tracer for HDAC2 imaging in the brain
blurb: New 18F PET tracer targeting histone deacetylase 2 in the brain. Whether HDAC2 is actually useful as an imaging biomarker in the clinic is still unclear, but having the tracer ready means the chemistry half is done if the target proves relevant.

original_title: Coated Bacterial Enzymes: A one-step approach for enzymatic purification and immobilization
title: One-step enzyme purification and immobilization via a bacterial surface binding domain
blurb: A bacterial cell wall binding domain (SlpA) as a fusion tag for one-pot purification and immobilization of recombinant enzymes, skipping chromatography. Proof of concept is beta-galactosidase from B. bifidum, so generalization and immobilization stability are the real questions.

original_title: Development of a novel xCT transporter imaging using a radiopharmaceutical with inhibitor-type compounds
title: A radiopharmaceutical targeting xCT transporter imaging
blurb: A new radiopharmaceutical targeting xCT (the cystine/glutamate transporter) for PET or SPECT imaging, using an inhibitor-type compound as the tracer. xCT's link to neuroinflammation makes it interesting, but whether imaging xCT status actually adds diagnostic value is still an open question.

original_title: Quantitative interactome mapping of skeletal muscle insulin resistance.
title: The muscle interactome in insulin resistance
blurb: A systems-level map of protein-protein interactions in insulin-resistant skeletal muscle, hunting for wiring changes that explain reduced glucose uptake. Interactome maps are useful reference work, but causation is a harder problem than correlation.
